info.name: PrimaryFeed
info.description:
PrimaryFeed Food Bank Management System – CS 5200 Group 6 (The Primary Keys)

## Setup
1. Import PrimaryFeed.postman_environment.json and select the PrimaryFeed environment.
2. Run Auth → Login (Staff) to populate {{staff_token}}.
3. Run Auth → Login (Volunteer) to populate {{volunteer_token}}.

## Notes
- Seed DML uses placeholder password hashes (hashed_pw_1, etc.) — not valid BCrypt.
  Update user records with real BCrypt hashes before login tests will pass.
- POST /api/shifts triggers a DB-level overlap check; a 400 with the trigger message is expected on conflict.
- food_sku env var defaults to SKU-001 (first item in seed data).

request.description:
Authenticate as staff user Alice Nguyen (user_id=1, branch 1). Saves {{staff_token}} on success.

⚠️ DML seed uses plain-text placeholder hashes — replace with a real BCrypt hash or use a user you seeded with bcrypt.
Authenticate as volunteer Carol Lee (user_id=3, volunteer_id=1, branch 2). Saves {{volunteer_token}} on success.
Expected: 401 {"error": "Invalid credentials"}
Returns the authenticated user's profile decoded from the JWT.
inventory_id=1 → SKU-001 at branch 1 (quantity 0, expiry +45 days from seed)
All inventory records at branch_id=1 (Downtown Boston).
Items where quantity > 0 and expiry_date is within the next 3 days.
Items where quantity = 0. Both seed inventory records should appear here.
Updates inventory_id=1. Body must include all required fields.
⚠️ Deletes inventory_id=2 (SKU-001, branch 1, quantity 0 from seed). Replace with a safe test ID.
Uses {{food_sku}} env var (default: SKU-001, Canned Chickpeas).
sku is the PK (VARCHAR 45) — must be unique. category.categoryId must reference an existing food_categories row.
Run Create Food Item first so SKU-TEST exists.
⚠️ Deletes SKU-TEST. Will 409/500 if inventory records reference this SKU (FK constraint).
Read-only. Category names drive the tiered expiry logic in vw_expiring_inventory — do not mutate.
category_id=1 → Canned Goods
donation_id=1: branch 1, donor John Carter, user Alice Nguyen, 2026-03-01
Donations at branch_id=1 (Downtown Boston). Seed has donations 1, 2, 9 here.
Donations from donor_id=1 (John Carter). Seed has donations 1 and 9.
user.userId references users.user_id (any role). branch, donor, and user must all exist.
⚠️ Deletes donation_id=10 (last seed record, branch 9, donor Whole Foods). Will fail with FK error if donation_items reference it — delete items first.
All line items for donation_id=1. Seed has 3 items: SKU-001 (120 cans), SKU-001 (38 cans, different expiry), SKU-011 (60 cans).
donation_item_id=1: donation 1, SKU-001, 120 cans, expiry 2026-12-31.
Unique key is (donation_id, food_sku, expiry_date). The expiryDate here differs from existing seed rows so it will not conflict.
⚠️ Deletes donation_item_id=14 (last seed item: donation 10, SKU-010, 12 tins).
distribution_id=1: branch 1, beneficiary Linda Park, user Alice Nguyen, 2026-03-03.
Distributions at branch_id=1. Seed has distributions 1, 2, 9.
Distributions for beneficiary_id=1 (Linda Park). Seed has distribution 1.
user.userId references users.user_id (any role — staff or volunteer may record).
⚠️ Deletes distribution_id=9 (branch 1, beneficiary Ruth Okafor). Delete its distribution_items first.
Line items for distribution_id=1. Seed: inventory_id 1 (5 units) and 4 (2 units).
distribution_item_id=1: distribution 1, inventory_id 1, quantity 5.
inventory_id references inventories.inventory_id directly (not food_sku). Unique on (distribution_id, inventory_id).
⚠️ Deletes distribution_item_id=13 (distribution 9, inventory 14, 3 units — SKU-011).
beneficiary_id=1: Linda Park, household 3, eligible.
Returns beneficiaries with eligibility_status=1. All 10 seed records are eligible (Nina Kowalski was updated to eligible in DML).
⚠️ Deletes beneficiary_id=10 (Ruth Okafor). Will fail if distributions reference this beneficiary — delete distributions first.
donor_id=1: John Carter, individual donor.
donorType: 0=Individual, 1=Organization. email must be unique.
⚠️ Deletes donor_id=8 (Trader Joe's). Will fail if donations reference this donor — delete donations first.
Seed has 4 volunteers: Carol Lee (1), Eva Patel (2), Grace Wang (3), Iris Chen (4).
volunteer_id=1: Carol Lee, weekends, background check cleared.
Volunteers whose user account is assigned to branch_id=2 (South End). Carol Lee (user_id=3) is at branch 2.

⚠️ user_id=2 (Bob Smith) is seeded as staff. Creating a volunteer profile for a staff user is allowed by the schema but creates an inconsistency. Use a userId that does not already have a volunteer or staff subtype record, or seed a new user first.
⚠️ Deletes volunteer_id=4 (Iris Chen, background_check=0). Delete any shifts for this volunteer first.
shift_id=1: volunteer 1 (Carol Lee), branch 1, 2026-04-05 09:00–12:00.
All shifts for volunteer_id=1 (Carol Lee).
Shifts at branch_id=1 (Downtown Boston).
Shifts on 2026-04-05. Seed has shifts for volunteers 1 and 2 on this date.
Creates a new shift for Carol Lee (volunteer_id=1) on a date with no existing shift — should succeed.

Expected: 400 {"error": "<trigger MESSAGE_TEXT>"}

Volunteer 1 already has a shift on 2026-04-12 09:00–12:00 at branch 1. This shift overlaps (11:00–14:00 at any branch) and should be rejected by trg_volunteer_shift_no_overlap_insert.
Extends shift_id=1 from 12:00 to 13:00. UPDATE trigger excludes the current row to avoid false self-conflict.
⚠️ Deletes shift_id=10 (volunteer 1, branch 1, 2026-04-05 12:00–15:00 — the back-to-back shift from seed).

item.name: Q1 — Food Items Available at Branch

request.description:
Returns food items with quantity > 0 at branch_id=1 (Downtown Boston).

Expected 403 if called with {{volunteer_token}}.

item.name: Q2 — Food Items Expiring Within 3 Days

request.description:
Items where expiry_date BETWEEN NOW() AND DATE_ADD(NOW(), INTERVAL 3 DAY) AND quantity > 0.

Seed has several items with expiry DATE_ADD(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 3 DAY) and DATE_ADD(CURDATE(), INTERVAL 4 DAY) — the 3-day ones (milk, potatoes batch, chicken) should appear.

item.name: Q3 — Total Quantity Across All Branches

request.description: SUM(quantity) GROUP BY food item across all branches. Excludes zero-stock rows.

item.name: Q4 — Food Items Grouped by Category

request.description: All food items joined to their category, ordered by category name then food name.

item.name: Q5 — Top Distributing Branch Last Month

request.description: CTE: sums distribution_items.quantity per branch for last calendar month. Returns LIMIT 1.
Active volunteers (users.status=1, role=1) at branch_id=2 (South End). Carol Lee should appear.
Reads directly from vw_volunteer_hours_log_last_30_days. Returns shifts from the last 30 days with formatted total_hours (e.g. 3h 0m).

item.name: Q8 — Volunteers at Branch in Time Window

request.description:
Volunteers at branch 1 whose shifts overlap the window 08:00–13:00 on 2026-04-05.
Overlap condition: shift_time_start < endTime AND shift_time_end > startTime.

Shift 1 (Carol Lee, 09:00–12:00) should match. Shift 10 (Carol Lee, 12:00–15:00) ends after 13:00 but starts at 12:00 < 13:00 so also matches.
All distributions for beneficiary_id=1 (Linda Park), with line-item details (food name, quantity, unit). Walks distributions → distribution_items → inventories → food_items.

item.name: Q10 — Beneficiaries Served per Branch This Week

request.description: COUNT(DISTINCT beneficiary_id) per branch for the current week (Sunday start via DAYOFWEEK).

item.name: Q11 — All Donations from a Donor

request.description: All donations from donor_id=1 (John Carter) with line-item detail. Seed: donations 1 and 9.
Two CTEs (received, distributed) joined to food_bank_branches. Returns total_received, total_distributed, net_surplus per branch. Positive surplus = more donated than distributed.
Runtime threshold — no hardcoded table. Returns inventory rows where quantity < threshold. Change threshold to 0 to see only out-of-stock, or 200 to see everything below 200 units.

item.name: Q14 — All Users and Their Roles

request.description: All users with human-readable role_name (Staff / Volunteer) and their branch. Note: password_hash is excluded from JSON via @JsonProperty(WRITE_ONLY).
Returns total_donated, total_distributed, total_activity per food category. Ordered by total_activity DESC.
Groups by CAST(distribution_date AS DATE) for the last 14 days. Returns distribution_day, total_quantity, num_distributions.
volunteer_count / distribution_count per branch. NULLIF prevents divide-by-zero. Branches with zero distributions get NULL ratio.
Uses {{volunteer_token}} against a reports endpoint. Expected: 403 Forbidden. Verifies that the STAFF-only gate (SecurityConfig + @PreAuthorize) is working.

item.name: Users
item.description: Staff-only. All endpoints require {{staff_token}}. POST creates the users row plus the matching staff or volunteers subtype row in one call.

request.description: Returns all users regardless of role. Staff-only.
user_id=1 → Alice Nguyen (staff, branch 1) from seed data.
role=0 for staff, role=1 for volunteers.
All users assigned to branch_id=1 (Downtown Boston).
Creates a users row (role=0) and a staff subtype row. Returns the saved User. The test script saves {{created_user_id}} for the update/delete tests below.
alice.nguyen@bafb.org is already in the seed data. Expected: 400 {"error": "Email already registered"}.
Creates a users row (role=1) and a volunteers subtype row.
Updates phone and status for the user created by 'Create Staff User'. Uses {{created_user_id}} saved by that test's post-response script. Role cannot be changed via this endpoint.
Re-hashes and persists a new password for the user. The passwordHash field is write-only and must not appear in the response.
Deletes the user created by 'Create Staff User'. Run after the update tests.
Confirms the user no longer exists after deletion.

exec: pm.test('Status 403 for volunteer on users endpoint', () => pm.response.to.have.status(403));

request.description: Volunteers must not access /api/users. Expected: 403 Forbidden.

item.name: Inventory Impact Chain
item.description:
End-to-end verification that donation → inventory → distribution works correctly.

Test subject: SKU-004 (White Rice 5lb, Grains) at branch_id=2 (South End).
Expiry used: 2099-12-31 — a deliberately far-future date so a guaranteed-fresh inventory row is created (no collision with seed data).

Expected state transitions:
  [no row] → +100 bags (donation) → 60 bags (−40 distribution) → 400 on −61 (insufficient)

Run IN ORDER — each request depends on the environment variables saved by the previous test script:
  {{inv_test_donation_id}}, {{inv_test_inv_id}}, {{inv_test_dist_id}}

exec: const existing = rows.filter(r => r.foodItem && r.foodItem.sku === 'SKU-004' && r.expiryDate && r.expiryDate.startsWith('2099'));
pm.test('No SKU-004/2099 row exists yet at branch 2 (clean slate)', () => {
pm.expect(existing.length, 'Run on a fresh DB or after cleanup').to.eql(0);

request.description: Captures the pre-donation state of branch 2 inventory. Verifies no SKU-004 row with expiry 2099-12-31 exists yet (would fail on re-run without DB cleanup).

Creates the donation header row.
branch_id=2 (South End), donor_id=2 (Whole Foods Market), user_id=1 (Alice Nguyen).

NO inventory change yet — the trigger lives on donation_items, not donations.

item.name: 3. Add Donation Item → Triggers Inventory Creation

request.description:
Inserts a donation_items row. This fires:
  trg_after_donation_items_insert

Trigger logic:
  1. JOIN donation → get branch_id=2
  2. Look for inventories WHERE food_sku='SKU-004' AND branch_id=2 AND unit='bags' AND expiry_date='2099-12-31'
  3. No match found → INSERT INTO inventories (SKU-004, branch_id=2, qty=100, 'bags', 2099-12-31)
  4. Logs to trigger_logs: 'Created new inventory batch for SKU: SKU-004'

Result: a brand-new inventory row is created with quantity=100.

item.name: 4. Verify Inventory Created (+100 bags) → Save inventory_id

exec: pm.test('SKU-004 inventory batch exists at branch 2', () => pm.expect(batch).to.not.be.undefined);
pm.test('Quantity is exactly 100 (trigger incremented from 0)', () => pm.expect(batch.quantity).to.eql(100));

request.description:
Re-fetches branch 2 inventory to confirm the trigger created the new row.
Finds the SKU-004 batch expiring 2099-12-31, asserts qty=100, and saves {{inv_test_inv_id}}.
Direct fetch of the newly created inventory row by ID. Confirms SKU, branch, quantity, and unit before we distribute from it.

Creates the distribution header row.
branch_id=2, beneficiary_id=2 (Marcus Johnson, household_size=5), user_id=1 (Alice).

NO inventory change yet — the trigger lives on distribution_items.

item.name: 7. Add Distribution Item (40 bags) → Triggers Inventory Decrement

request.description:
Inserts a distribution_items row referencing inventory_id={{inv_test_inv_id}}. This fires:
  trg_after_distribution_items_insert

Trigger logic:
  1. Check: inventories.quantity (100) >= requested (40)? YES → proceed
  2. UPDATE inventories SET quantity = 100 - 40 = 60 WHERE inventory_id = {{inv_test_inv_id}}
  3. Logs to trigger_logs: 'Decremented inventory_id: X by 40'

Result: inventory row quantity drops from 100 → 60.

exec: pm.test('Quantity is now 60 (100 donated − 40 distributed)', () => pm.expect(inv.quantity).to.eql(60));

request.description: Confirms the trigger fired correctly. Expected quantity = 60.

item.name: 9. Create Second Distribution Header (for overflow test)

request.description:
WHY a new header is needed:

distribution_items has UNIQUE KEY uq_distribution_inventory (distribution_id, inventory_id).
Within one distribution event (header), each inventory batch can appear exactly once — re-inserting the same (distribution_id, inventory_id) pair would be a duplicate entry, not an overflow error.

Each distribution header = one real-world transaction (one beneficiary visit). To take more from the same batch you create a new transaction. This is correct by design.

This header uses beneficiary_id=3 (Sofia Reyes) to represent a second distribution event.

New distribution header, same inventory batch. Only 60 bags remain (100 donated − 40 distributed).

Trigger logic:
  1. Check: inventories.quantity (60) >= requested (61)? NO
  2. SIGNAL SQLSTATE '45000' 'Insufficient stock: quantity would go below 0.'

Spring → DataIntegrityViolationException → GlobalExceptionHandler → 400.

item.name: 11. Confirm Inventory Unchanged After Failed Distribution (still 60)

exec: pm.test('Quantity still 60 — trigger abort prevented decrement', () => pm.expect(inv.quantity).to.eql(60));

request.description: Confirms the insufficient-stock trigger abort left inventory intact. Quantity must still be 60.
